items.title: Bayes' theorem
items.description: Prior/posterior updating is Bayes' theorem applied to parameters: P(θ|x) = P(x|θ)P(θ)/P(x).

items.title: Maximum likelihood estimation
items.description: MLE maximizes P(x|θ) alone; with a broad (near flat) prior, MAP/posterior mode aligns with MLE, but priors matter when data are scarce or regularization is desired.

items.title: Ridge and Lasso penalties
items.description: Gaussian priors on coefficients correspond to Ridge (L2); Laplace priors correspond to Lasso (L1)—MAP under those priors equals penalized least squares.

items.title: Evidence lower bound (ELBO)
items.description: When the true posterior is intractable, variational methods optimize a lower bound on log evidence—still rooted in the same posterior P(θ|x) target.
